title: Aleuritia fedtschenkoi
taxonomy: Aleuritia fedtschenkoi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the genus Aleuritia and is native to Asia. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Within the family, Aleuritia fedtschenkoi is part of the order Lamiales, which includes many other flowering plant families. The classification of Aleuritia fedtschenkoi is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants.

intro: Aleuritia fedtschenkoi, also known as Chinese angelica or dong quai substitute, is a plant species in the family Apiaceae. It is used in traditional medicine to promote women's health, alleviate menstrual cramps, and support immune function due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Aleuritia fedtschenkoi include coumarins, alkaloids, and volatile oils, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Aleuritia fedtschenkoi is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion and is often used as a substitute for dong quai, although its efficacy and safety may vary compared to the more well-studied and widely used Chinese angelica.
